components.title: Accessibility Label
components.summary: Overrides the short, localized name VoiceOver reads for an element, replacing any inferred label.

properties.name: label
properties.notes: The spoken name. Keep it short, a noun phrase, no control type word (no 'button').

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: accessibilityLabel(_:isEnabled:) overload lets you conditionally apply the label.

properties.name: targetContent
properties.notes: Demonstrates the case where a label is mandatory: icon-only controls have no inferable text.

components.higNotes: Every actionable, icon-only, or image element needs a concise label. Do not include the trait noun ('button', 'image') — VoiceOver appends it from traits. Capitalize like a label, omit ending punctuation.

components.title: Accessibility Value
components.summary: Supplies the current value VoiceOver reads after the label, for elements whose state changes.

properties.name: value
properties.notes: Current value spoken after label and before traits. Use human phrasing ('40 percent' not '0.4').

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: accessibilityValue(_:isEnabled:) overload conditionally applies the value.

components.higNotes: Provide a value only when the element has dynamic state (slider %, progress, stepper count). Pair with an adjustable action so VoiceOver users can change it. Spell numbers as people would say them.

components.title: Accessibility Hint
components.summary: Adds a longer description of what activating an element does; spoken after a pause if hints are enabled.

properties.name: hint
properties.notes: Action-result phrasing, full sentence. Spoken only when VoiceOver hints are on.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: accessibilityHint(_:isEnabled:) overload conditionally applies the hint.

components.higNotes: Use sparingly — hints are a fallback, not the primary description. Start with a verb describing the outcome ('Plays the song'). Never duplicate the label. Users can disable hints, so do not put essential info here.

components.title: Accessibility Traits
components.summary: Describes how an element behaves so assistive tech speaks the right role and offers the right gestures.

properties.name: addedTraits
properties.notes: OptionSet; combine with array literal. isToggle added iOS 17. Use isHeader for section titles to enable heading navigation.

properties.name: removedTraits
properties.notes: accessibilityRemoveTraits strips an inferred trait, e.g. remove isImage from a decorative glyph styled as text.

components.higNotes: Apply traits that match real behavior. isHeader powers the rotor's Headings navigation. isSelected marks the active item in a set. Do not fake isButton on non-interactive content. Prefer native controls that supply traits automatically.

components.title: Accessibility Input Labels
components.summary: Provides alternate spoken names Voice Control and Switch Control accept to target an element.

properties.name: inputLabels
properties.notes: Ordered by preference; the first is the primary Voice Control phrase. Offer natural alternatives users might say.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: accessibilityInputLabels(_:isEnabled:) overload conditionally applies them.

components.higNotes: Add input labels when the visible label differs from what a Voice Control user would say, or for icon-only controls. Keep phrases short and speakable; avoid punctuation and symbols.

components.title: Accessibility Hidden
components.summary: Removes an element and its children from the accessibility tree without affecting visual layout.

properties.name: hidden
properties.notes: When true the element is invisible to VoiceOver. Use for purely decorative imagery or duplicated content.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: accessibilityHidden(_:isEnabled:) overload conditionally applies the hiding.

components.higNotes: Hide only decorative or redundant elements. Never hide interactive controls or essential information. Prefer combining children into one element over hiding meaningful content.

components.title: Accessibility Element (Children Behavior)
components.summary: Merges a subtree into a single element or controls how its children are exposed to assistive tech.

properties.name: children
properties.notes: .ignore makes a single opaque element (default); .combine merges children's labels/traits/actions; .contain keeps children navigable as a container.

components.higNotes: Combine a card's stacked Text/Image into one swipe stop with .combine so VoiceOver reads it as a unit. Use .contain for scroll containers and grids that should remain individually navigable. Avoid .ignore when children carry distinct meaning.

components.title: Accessibility Children (Synthetic)
components.summary: Replaces an element's children with synthetic, non-visual accessibility elements you build in a ViewBuilder.

properties.name: children
properties.notes: ViewBuilder content; SwiftUI hides the views visually and uses them only to generate a11y elements. Ideal for Canvas / custom drawing.

components.higNotes: Use for custom-drawn content (Canvas, GraphicsContext) that has no inherent elements. Give each synthetic child a clear label and value so VoiceOver can navigate the data points.

components.title: Accessibility Representation
components.summary: Substitutes a custom view's accessibility with that of a standard control for richer assistive behavior.

properties.name: representation
properties.notes: Returns a standard control whose full accessibility (traits, adjustable actions) is borrowed by the custom view.

components.higNotes: Reach for this when you build a custom control that mimics a system one — wrap a hidden Slider/Toggle so users get the native adjustable gestures and value semantics for free.

components.title: Dynamic Type Size
components.summary: Constrains or overrides the Dynamic Type size for a subtree and lets you read the user's current size.

properties.name: size
properties.notes: Single value pins the size; .large is the system default. accessibility1-5 are the larger AX sizes.

properties.name: range
properties.notes: dynamicTypeSize(_:) range overload clamps user choice. Never hard-cap below accessibility5 unless layout truly cannot adapt.

properties.name: isAccessibilitySize
properties.notes: Read-only computed on DynamicTypeSize; true for accessibility1-5. Use to switch to a stacked layout at AX sizes.

components.higNotes: Support all sizes through accessibility5. Use semantic text styles so text scales automatically. Switch HStack→VStack at .isAccessibilitySize. Avoid clamping ranges unless a fixed-size element (e.g. a watch complication) demands it.

components.title: ScaledMetric
components.summary: Property wrapper that scales a numeric value (padding, icon size) in step with the user's Dynamic Type size.

properties.name: wrappedValue
properties.notes: The base value at the default text size; the wrapper returns a scaled result. Common for icon frames and spacing.

properties.name: relativeTo
properties.notes: init(wrappedValue:relativeTo:) ties scaling to a specific text style's metrics rather than the default body.

components.higNotes: Scale non-text dimensions (icon size, custom row height, spacing) so they grow with text. Anchor with relativeTo: matching the adjacent text style. Do not scale fixed-asset dimensions that would break layout (e.g. hairline dividers).

components.title: Accessibility Action
components.summary: Adds default, escape, magic-tap, or named custom actions VoiceOver and Switch Control can invoke.

properties.name: kind
properties.notes: Standard action slot. .escape dismisses (two-finger scrub), .magicTap is the global two-finger double-tap, .showMenu opens context menu.

properties.name: name
properties.notes: accessibilityAction(named:_:) creates a named custom action surfaced in the VoiceOver actions rotor.

properties.name: intent
properties.notes: accessibilityAction(_:intent:) / (named:intent:) wire an action to an App Intent for system integration (iOS 18+).

components.higNotes: Expose every gesture-only action (swipe-to-delete, drag) as a named accessibility action so it is reachable without the gesture. Reserve .magicTap for the single most important action of a screen.

components.title: Accessibility Adjustable Action
components.summary: Handles VoiceOver swipe-up/down increment and decrement gestures for custom value controls.

properties.name: handler
properties.notes: Direction is .increment or .decrement. Update the bound value and keep accessibilityValue in sync.

components.higNotes: Pair with accessibilityValue so VoiceOver announces the new value after each adjustment. Use for custom sliders, ratings, segmented pickers, and steppers built from scratch.

components.title: Accessibility Scroll Action
components.summary: Responds to VoiceOver's three-finger scroll on custom paginated or carousel content.

properties.name: handler
properties.notes: Edge indicates scroll direction; advance your page/index accordingly. Built-in scroll views handle this automatically.

components.higNotes: Only needed for custom scroll/paging containers that do not use ScrollView/TabView. Move to the correct page based on the edge and post a focus/announcement update.

components.title: Accessibility Rotor
components.summary: Adds a custom VoiceOver rotor letting users jump directly to a curated set of elements or text ranges.

properties.name: rotorLabel
properties.notes: User-visible rotor name shown in the VoiceOver rotor wheel.

properties.name: entries
properties.notes: Entry IDs must match accessibilityRotorEntry / ForEach IDs in the same ScrollView so SwiftUI can scroll them into view.

properties.name: entryLabel
properties.notes: Key path to a user-visible label per entry; shown on macOS when listing rotor entries.

properties.name: systemRotor
properties.notes: Use the AccessibilitySystemRotor overload to replace a built-in rotor when default detection misses off-screen elements.

properties.name: note-overloads
properties.notes: The draft only models accessibilityRotor(_:entries:). Apple ships four overloads: accessibilityRotor(_:entries:), (_:entries:entryID:entryLabel:), (_:entries:entryLabel:), and (_:textRanges:) for text-range rotors. The textRanges overload is distinct enough to mention; consider adding a 'rotorKind' picker [element entries, text ranges].

components.higNotes: Attach to a ScrollView or an element directly inside one. Provide rotors for content users frequently seek (unread, VIPs, headings) — especially items lazily loaded off-screen that the system rotor cannot find.

components.title: Accessibility Focus State
components.summary: Reads and programmatically moves VoiceOver/Switch Control focus to a specific element.

properties.name: binding
properties.notes: accessibilityFocused(_:) Bool overload. Set the state to true to move focus here; reads true when focused.

properties.name: equalsValue
properties.notes: accessibilityFocused(_:equals:) drives focus among many elements via a shared @AccessibilityFocusState enum.

components.higNotes: Move focus only in response to meaningful events — surface a validation error, reveal new content, dismiss a sheet. Avoid stealing focus during routine updates; it disorients assistive-tech users.

components.title: Accessibility Custom Content
components.summary: Attaches extra label/value pairs that VoiceOver users can expand on demand via the More Content rotor.

properties.name: label
properties.notes: Short name for the extra detail. Reuse an AccessibilityCustomContentKey to update the same slot.

properties.name: value
properties.notes: The detail value spoken when the user expands custom content.

properties.name: importance
properties.notes: .high content is spoken automatically; .default is spoken only when the user requests more content.

components.higNotes: Use to keep the primary label concise while still offering rich secondary detail (flight times, metadata) without overwhelming every swipe. Mark only truly essential extras as .high.

components.title: Accessibility Heading
components.summary: Marks an element as a heading at a given level so VoiceOver's heading rotor can navigate the hierarchy.

properties.name: level
properties.notes: Establishes a heading level for hierarchical navigation. Combine with isHeader trait for full support.

components.higNotes: Mark section titles as headings so users can skim with the Headings rotor. Keep levels consistent and nested correctly (h1 then h2), mirroring the visual hierarchy.

components.title: Accessibility Text Content Type
components.summary: Tells VoiceOver the semantic kind of text so it adapts navigation and speech to that content.

properties.name: value
properties.notes: Hints how text should be navigated/spoken — e.g. .sourceCode reads punctuation, .messaging groups conversation turns.

components.higNotes: Set on large or specialized text views (code editors, chat transcripts, documents) so VoiceOver granularity and punctuation handling match the content domain.

components.title: Reduce Motion
components.summary: Read-only flag indicating the user prefers minimal animation; gate motion-heavy effects behind it.

properties.name: reduceMotion
properties.notes: Read via @Environment(\.accessibilityReduceMotion). When true, replace parallax/zoom/looping animation with a cross-fade or none.

components.higNotes: Honor Reduce Motion for any large, looping, parallax, or spring animation. Provide a subtle cross-fade or instant change instead. Essential affordance animations may remain if they are small and non-disorienting.

components.title: Reduce Transparency
components.summary: Read-only flag indicating the user prefers opaque backgrounds over translucent materials.

properties.name: reduceTransparency
properties.notes: Read via @Environment(\.accessibilityReduceTransparency). When true, swap .ultraThinMaterial / glass for a solid fill.

components.higNotes: Provide an opaque fallback for every blurred/material/glass surface. Translucency reduces legibility; respecting this preference is mandatory for backgrounds behind text.

components.title: Differentiate Without Color
components.summary: Read-only flag indicating the user needs cues beyond color (shapes, icons, text) to distinguish meaning.

properties.name: differentiateWithoutColor
properties.notes: Read via @Environment(\.accessibilityDifferentiateWithoutColor). When true add a glyph/shape/label alongside any color-coded status.

components.higNotes: Never rely on color alone to convey state (success/error, on/off, selected). When this is true, add a checkmark, shape, pattern, or text label so color-blind users get the same information.

components.title: Increase / Differentiate Contrast
components.summary: Read-only flag (.standard / .increased) reflecting the user's Increase Contrast preference.

properties.name: colorSchemeContrast
properties.notes: Read via @Environment(\.colorSchemeContrast). When .increased, raise foreground/background contrast and strengthen borders.

components.higNotes: When contrast is increased, use higher-contrast colors, add borders to controls, and avoid low-opacity text. Prefer asset-catalog 'High Contrast' color variants so the system swaps them automatically.

components.title: Invert Colors Handling
components.summary: Excludes media from Smart Invert and reports whether Invert Colors is active.

properties.name: ignoresInvert
properties.notes: accessibilityIgnoresInvertColors(_:) exempts photos/video/icons from color inversion under Smart Invert.

properties.name: invertColors
properties.notes: Read-only @Environment(\.accessibilityInvertColors); true when the user enabled Invert Colors.

components.higNotes: Apply accessibilityIgnoresInvertColors to images, video, and color-critical content so Smart Invert does not produce wrong colors. Leave text and UI chrome subject to inversion.

components.title: Button Shapes / On-Off Labels
components.summary: Read-only flag indicating the user wants visible shapes around tappable text-style buttons.

properties.name: showButtonShapes
properties.notes: Read via @Environment(\.accessibilityShowButtonShapes). When true, add an explicit background/border to plain text buttons.

components.higNotes: Native button styles honor this automatically. For custom borderless/plain buttons, add a visible shape (background, underline, border) when the flag is true so users can tell what is tappable.

components.title: Large Content Viewer
components.summary: Shows an enlarged HUD of an element's label/image when a Larger Text user long-presses it.

properties.name: enabled
properties.notes: accessibilityShowsLargeContentViewer() opts an element into the large-content HUD.

properties.name: label
properties.notes: accessibilityShowsLargeContentViewer { } content builder customizes the enlarged label/icon shown in the HUD.

properties.name: isLargeContentViewerEnabled
properties.notes: Read-only @Environment(\.accessibilityLargeContentViewerEnabled) reports whether the system viewer is active.

components.higNotes: Use for small fixed-size controls that cannot scale — tab bar items, toolbar icons. The HUD lets Larger Text users read them on long-press without resizing the control itself.

components.title: Accessibility Sort Priority
components.summary: Adjusts the order in which VoiceOver visits elements within a container, higher first.

properties.name: priority
properties.notes: Higher priority is visited earlier. Default 0. Use to surface a primary action ahead of decorative or secondary elements.

components.higNotes: Override reading order only when the visual order misleads (e.g. a floating action button should be read after the list, or an alert title before its body). Keep it consistent with logical importance.

components.title: Accessibility Activation Point
components.summary: Specifies the exact screen point assistive tech uses when activating a combined or custom element.

properties.name: point
properties.notes: UnitPoint overload maps relative to the element's frame. Use when the default center would miss the real hit target.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: accessibilityActivationPoint(_:isEnabled:) conditionally applies the custom activation point.

components.higNotes: Set when a combined element's tappable region is not at its center — e.g. a row whose only control is a trailing button. Ensures VoiceOver double-tap hits the intended target.

components.title: Accessibility Direct Touch
components.summary: Lets VoiceOver pass touches straight through to a region (e.g. a piano keyboard or drawing canvas).

properties.name: isDirectTouchArea
properties.notes: Marks the region for direct touch so gestures reach your view instead of VoiceOver.

properties.name: options
properties.notes: .requiresActivation makes the user double-tap to engage; .silentOnTouch mutes VoiceOver while interacting.

components.higNotes: Use only for instrument-like, gesture-driven surfaces where per-element VoiceOver navigation makes no sense. Prefer .requiresActivation so users opt in deliberately, and always offer an accessible alternative.

components.title: Accessibility Zoom Action
components.summary: Handles VoiceOver's zoom gesture so users can magnify custom zoomable content.

properties.name: handler
properties.notes: The action carries .direction (.zoomIn/.zoomOut) and a magnification value; update your zoom level and announce it.

components.higNotes: Add to custom maps, image viewers, or canvases that support pinch-to-zoom so VoiceOver users get an equivalent zoom control. Update accessibilityValue with the new zoom level.

components.title: Accessibility Drag & Drop Points
components.summary: Exposes drag-and-drop targets to assistive tech as discrete, described points.

properties.name: dragPoint
properties.notes: accessibilityDragPoint(_:description:) marks where a drag begins for the element.

properties.name: dropPoint
properties.notes: accessibilityDropPoint(_:description:) marks a valid drop location with a spoken description.

properties.name: description
properties.notes: Spoken description of what the drag or drop accomplishes.

properties.name: minOS-correction
properties.notes: CORRECTION: accessibilityDragPoint/DropPoint are iOS 16.0+ (verified), not iOS 18.0 as drafted. Correct platforms: iOS 16.0+, iPadOS 16.0+, Mac Catalyst 16.0+, macOS 13.0+, visionOS 1.0+. No watchOS, no tvOS. Draft platforms [iOS, iPadOS, macOS] are otherwise fine but minOS should be 'iOS 16.0'. There is also an isEnabled overload: accessibilityDragPoint(_:description:isEnabled:) / accessibilityDropPoint(_:description:isEnabled:) — add an isEnabled toggle property.

components.higNotes: Make every drag-and-drop interaction reachable without precise pointing. Describe the outcome clearly ('Drop on Trash to delete') and provide both a drag origin and each meaningful drop target.

components.title: Accessibility Quick Action
components.summary: Surfaces a quick action (prompt or outline) that assistive-tech users can trigger contextually.

properties.name: style
properties.notes: .prompt presents a confirming prompt; .outline highlights the affected region before the action runs.

properties.name: isActive
properties.notes: accessibilityQuickAction(style:isActive:content:) gates whether the quick action is currently offered.

properties.name: content
properties.notes: ViewBuilder of the controls offered in the quick action surface.

properties.name: platforms-correction
properties.notes: CORRECTION: accessibilityQuickAction(style:content:) and (style:isActive:content:) are watchOS 9.0+ ONLY per Apple docs (verified). The draft's platforms array [iOS, iPadOS, macOS, watchOS] and minOS 'iOS 18.0' are WRONG. Set platforms to [watchOS] and minOS to 'watchOS 9.0'.

components.higNotes: Offer quick actions for the highest-value contextual operations on a screen. Keep the action set short and label each clearly so assistive-tech users can act without deep navigation.

components.title: Accessibility Identifier
components.summary: Sets a stable, non-localized identifier used by UI tests and automation, not spoken to users.

properties.name: identifier
properties.notes: Stable test identifier. Never localized; not announced by VoiceOver. Keep unique within a screen.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: accessibilityIdentifier(_:isEnabled:) conditionally applies the identifier.

components.higNotes: Use kebab- or dot-namespaced identifiers for XCUITest targeting. This is an automation hook, not a user-facing label — do not put spoken content here.

components.title: Responds To User Interaction
components.summary: Declares whether an element is interactive, so assistive tech can correctly group or skip it.

properties.name: respondsToUserInteraction
properties.notes: Set false for a combined element that looks tappable but is purely informational, so VoiceOver does not imply interactivity.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: accessibilityRespondsToUserInteraction(_:isEnabled:) conditionally applies the value.

components.higNotes: Use to correct mismatches after combining children: if a merged card has no action, mark it non-interactive so VoiceOver does not suggest a double-tap. Keep in sync with real behavior.

components.title: Speech Options
components.summary: Fine-tunes how VoiceOver pronounces an element: spelling, pitch, punctuation, and queueing.

properties.name: spellsOutCharacters
properties.notes: When true VoiceOver reads each character individually — ideal for codes, license plates, serials.

properties.name: adjustedPitch
properties.notes: Raises (positive) or lowers (negative) speech pitch to signal emphasis or context changes.

properties.name: alwaysIncludesPunctuation
properties.notes: Forces punctuation to be spoken regardless of the user's verbosity setting — useful for code.

properties.name: announcementsQueued
properties.notes: When true, announcements queue instead of interrupting the current utterance.

components.higNotes: Use spellsOutCharacters for opaque alphanumeric strings, pitch changes to mark a status, and includesPunctuation for code or formulas. Apply sparingly — overuse makes speech tiring.

components.title: Accessibility Chart Descriptor
components.summary: Provides an audio-graph and data-table description so VoiceOver users can explore a chart's data.

properties.name: descriptor
properties.notes: Conform a type to AXChartDescriptorRepresentable describing axes, series, and data points for VoiceOver's Audio Graph.

components.higNotes: Make every data visualization explorable: define axes with titles and units, series with data points, so VoiceOver can play the audio graph and read the data table. Required for Swift Charts that convey non-trivial data.

components.title: Accessibility Labeled Pair
components.summary: Associates a label view with the content it describes so VoiceOver reads them as a coherent pair.

properties.name: role
properties.notes: Mark one view as .label and its partner as .content; they share a Namespace id to be linked.

properties.name: id
properties.notes: Shared identifier matching the label and its content within the same Namespace.

components.higNotes: Use when a label and value are laid out apart (e.g. a form column or grid) so VoiceOver still announces them together. Keep ids unique per pair within the namespace.

components.title: Accessibility Rotor Entry
components.summary: Tags a view so a custom rotor (defined elsewhere) can locate and scroll to it, even when the element is off-screen or in a different part of the hierarchy.

properties.name: id
properties.notes: Identifier matching an entry's id passed to accessibilityRotor(_:entries:). Lets SwiftUI scroll the tagged view into view when the rotor navigates to it.

properties.name: namespace
properties.notes: Shared @Namespace tying the entry to the rotor definition. The rotor and its entries must use the same namespace.

components.higNotes: Pair with accessibilityRotor when the elements a rotor references are lazily loaded or live far from the rotor's host. The id/namespace pair lets the system bring the right element on-screen and move VoiceOver focus to it. Without it, custom rotors cannot reliably reach off-screen content in a LazyVStack.

components.title: Accessibility Linked Group
components.summary: Links accessibility elements that are far apart in the hierarchy so users can quickly jump between them (e.g. a list item and its detail view).

properties.name: id
properties.notes: Identifier separating sets of linked elements within one namespace. Elements with matching id and namespace are linked.

properties.name: namespace
properties.notes: Shared @Namespace organizing the linked elements. All views marked with this namespace and id link together.

components.higNotes: Use to let assistive-tech users hop between the same content shown in two places — a row in a list and the same item in a detail pane. Keep ids unique per logical group within the namespace.

components.title: Accessibility Label (View Builder)
components.summary: Supplies an element's accessibility label from a view (rather than a string), so labels can be composed from other views including localized Text.

properties.name: content
properties.notes: ViewBuilder returning the view(s) whose text becomes the spoken label. Receives a placeholder representing the labeled view; lets you build the label from existing views instead of a literal string.

components.higNotes: Reach for the ViewBuilder overload when the label is best assembled from views you already have (e.g. concatenating several Text values) rather than a single string literal. The string/Text/LocalizedStringKey overloads remain the common case; this one avoids manually flattening composed content into one string.
